title: Substring with At Least K Repeats and Swaps

statement.markdown: Given two strings `s` and `t`, return all the starting indices of `substring(t)` that `s` can be rearranged into.  If there are no such indices, return an empty list.  A black box is given which allows you to get any substring of `t` of length at most 11.  You can call this black box at most k times as k is the number of characters in t.  You should return the indices in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided in the format: `string s, int n, int k` where `n` is the length of `t` and `k` is the number of swaps allowed.
statement.outputFormat: The method should return a list of integers representing the starting indices of `substring(t)` that `s` can be rearranged into.
statement.notes: The black box has a time limit of 2 seconds.  The length of `t` is at most 1000.  The length of `s` is at most 20000.  The length of `t` is at most 11.

examples.explanation: The substring "ab" can be rearranged into "ab" which is a substring of "ab"
The substrings "ab" and "ba" can be rearranged into "ab" which is a substring of "abab"
